title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install required packages for SMAK-CP experiments including numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, pandas, matplotlib, and seaborn for visualization.

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install core dependencies: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn pandas matplotlib seaborn tqdm
steps.step3: Verify installation by importing numpy, scipy, sklearn in Python
steps.step4: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data results figures logs

title: Generate and Prepare Synthetic Datasets
description: Create synthetic datasets with controlled heteroscedasticity and distribution drift for controlled evaluation.

steps.step1: Generate Synthetic Heteroscedastic dataset: Y = sin(2πX) + σ(X)·ε where σ(X) = 0.1 + 0.5·|X|, X ~ Uniform(-1,1), ε ~ N(0,1). Generate n=3000 samples. Save as data/synthetic_hetero.csv.
steps.step2: Generate Synthetic Distribution Drift dataset: Piecewise stationary with 4 segments of 750 samples each (total n=3000). Segment 1: Y = X + ε. Segment 2: Y = 2X + 2ε. Segment 3: Y = -X + 0.5ε. Segment 4: Y = 0.5X + ε. Save as data/synthetic_drift.csv.
steps.step3: Generate Synthetic Density-Varying dataset: Mixture of high-density (70% N(0,0.1)) and low-density (30% N(2,1)) regions. Y = sin(X) + 0.1·ε, n=3000. Save as data/synthetic_density.csv.
steps.step4: Compute and save dataset statistics to data/dataset_stats.json.

title: Download and Prepare Real-World UCI Datasets
description: Download and prepare smaller UCI regression datasets for realistic evaluation.

steps.step1: Download Bike Sharing dataset (hour.csv) from UCI. Target: cnt. Features: temp, atemp, hum, windspeed, hr. Use first 5000 samples sorted by datetime. Save as data/bike_sharing.csv.
steps.step2: Download Concrete Compressive Strength dataset from UCI. Use all 1030 samples. Target: strength. 8 normalized features. Save as data/concrete.csv.
steps.step3: Download Air Quality dataset from UCI. Use CO(GT) as target, 5 sensor features. Remove missing values, use first 3000 samples. Save as data/air_quality.csv.
steps.step4: Normalize all features to [0,1] using MinMaxScaler fitted on first 20% of data. Save scalers to data/scalers.pkl.
steps.step5: Create metadata files for each dataset with n_samples, n_features, temporal flag.

title: Implement and Run Split Conformal Prediction Baseline
description: Simple baseline: standard split conformal prediction with fixed quantile on calibration set. No adaptation.

steps.step1: Implement split_cp.py with class SplitConformalPredictor. Parameters: alpha=0.1, calibration_ratio=0.2.
steps.step2: Method: Train Ridge regression on training set. Compute non-conformity scores |Y - Ŷ| on calibration set. Use (1-alpha) quantile as threshold.
steps.step3: For streaming evaluation: at each step t, predict interval [Ŷ - q, Ŷ + q] where q is fixed from calibration. Compute coverage indicator.
steps.step4: Run on all 6 datasets with seeds [0, 1, 2]. Save results to results/split_cp_{dataset}_seed{seed}.json with fields: coverage_history, set_width_history, avg_coverage, avg_width.
steps.step5: Compute metrics: marginal coverage, average set width.

title: Implement and Run ACI (Adaptive Conformal Inference) Baseline
description: Strong online baseline: Gibbs & Candès 2021 ACI method with online miscoverage level adaptation.

steps.step1: Implement aci.py with class ACIPredictor. Parameters: alpha=0.1, gamma=0.05 (adaptation rate).
steps.step2: Method: Update quantile with learning rate gamma: q_{t+1} = q_t + gamma·(alpha - 1{y_t ∉ C_t}).
steps.step3: Use same underlying Ridge predictor as Split CP for fair comparison.
steps.step4: Run on all 6 datasets with seeds [0, 1, 2]. Save results to results/aci_{dataset}_seed{seed}.json.
steps.step5: Compute metrics: marginal coverage, MSCE (10 uniform bins), average set width.

title: Implement and Run Fixed-Bandwidth Kernel CP Baseline
description: Single-scale kernel-based CP with fixed bandwidth (represents standard kernel CP methods like RLCP without adaptation).

steps.step1: Implement fixed_kernel_cp.py with class FixedKernelCP. Parameters: alpha=0.1, bandwidth=0.1 (fixed), window_size=500.
steps.step2: Method: At each step t, compute kernel weights w_i = exp(-||X_i - X_t||²/(2h²)) for i in recent window. Compute weighted quantile.
steps.step3: Run on all 6 datasets with seeds [0, 1, 2]. Use bandwidth=0.1 (tuned on small validation).
steps.step4: Save results to results/fixed_kernel_cp_{dataset}_seed{seed}.json. Compute: marginal coverage, MSCE, WSC (10 bins), avg width.
steps.step5: This is the key comparison baseline representing fixed-bandwidth methods.

title: Implement SMAK-CP Core Framework
description: Implement the full SMAK-CP algorithm with multi-scale kernel hierarchy and online bandwidth adaptation.

steps.step1: Implement smak_cp.py with class SMAKCP. Parameters: alpha=0.1, K=4 scales, rho=2.0, h0=0.1, eta=0.05, window_size=500, n_min=20, T0=100, lambda_reg=0.1.
steps.step2: Multi-scale hierarchy: bandwidths h^(k) = h0 · rho^(k-1) for k=1..K → [0.1, 0.2, 0.4, 0.8].
steps.step3: Online adaptation: Update base bandwidth via log(h_{t+1}) = log(h_t) - eta · sign(sum_k w_t^(k) · Delta_t^(k)).
steps.step4: Hierarchical warm-up: t ≤ T0 fixed, T0 < t ≤ 2·T0 gradual, t > 2·T0 full adaptation.
steps.step5: Scale reliability: w_t^(k) ∝ exp(-lambda_reg · sum |Delta_s^(k)|). Fallback to k=1 if no scale has n_eff ≥ n_min.

title: Run SMAK-S (Single-Scale Selection) Variant
description: SMAK-CP with single-scale selection based on maximum effective sample size. This is the main proposed method.

steps.step1: Selection: k* = argmax_k n_t^(k)(X_t) among scales with n_t^(k) ≥ n_min.
steps.step2: Prediction: Form interval using quantile from selected scale k*.
steps.step3: Run on all 6 datasets with seeds [0, 1, 2]. Same hyperparameters across all runs.
steps.step4: Save detailed logs: selected_scale_history, bandwidth_history, coverage_discrepancy_history.
steps.step5: Save results to results/smak_s_{dataset}_seed{seed}.json with: marginal_coverage, MSCE, WSC, avg_width, runtime.
steps.step6: Track coverage in 100-step windows for convergence analysis.

title: Run SMAK-W (Weighted Aggregation) Variant
description: SMAK-CP with weighted combination of quantiles across all active scales.

steps.step1: Weighted aggregation: q_agg = sum_{k in A_t} w_tilde^(k) · q^(k) with normalized reliability weights.
steps.step2: Prediction: Form interval using aggregated quantile q_agg.
steps.step3: Run on all 6 datasets with seeds [0, 1, 2].
steps.step4: Save results to results/smak_w_{dataset}_seed{seed}.json with all metrics.
steps.step5: Compare coverage variance against SMAK-S to assess stability.

title: Run SMAK-I (Multi-Scale Intersection) Variant
description: SMAK-CP with calibrated intersection of prediction sets from multiple scales.

steps.step1: Calibrated intersection: alpha_k = alpha · w^(k) / sum_j w^(j) (adaptive calibration).
steps.step2: Form per-scale intervals C^(k) = [Ŷ - q^(k), Ŷ + q^(k)] with scale-specific quantiles.
steps.step3: Intersection: C = intersection_{k in A_t} C^(k).
steps.step4: Run on all 6 datasets with seeds [0, 1, 2].
steps.step6: Check for overly conservative sets (avg width > 2x SMAK-S). Report if intersection hurts efficiency.

title: Ablation: Fixed vs Adaptive Bandwidth
description: Test contribution of online bandwidth adaptation by comparing against fixed-bandwidth version of SMAK-S.

steps.step1: Implement SMAK-S-Fixed: Same as SMAK-S but disable bandwidth adaptation after warmup. Keep bandwidth fixed at final warmup value.
steps.step2: Run on 2 key datasets: synthetic_drift (shows adaptation benefit) and synthetic_hetero.
steps.step3: Use seed 0 only (ablation focused on mechanism, not statistical significance).
steps.step4: Compute: coverage drift over time (50-step windows), MSCE in different segments.
steps.step6: Test: Adaptive bandwidth reduces coverage deviation during drift periods.

title: Ablation: Multi-Scale vs Single-Scale
description: Test contribution of multi-scale structure by comparing against single-scale.

steps.step1: Implement SMAK-SingleScale: K=1, bandwidth fixed at 0.28 (geometric mean of multi-scale).
steps.step2: Run on 2 datasets: synthetic_density (tests density adaptation) and bike_sharing.
steps.step3: Use seed 0 only.
steps.step4: Analyze by density: Compare MSCE in high-density vs low-density regions.
steps.step6: Test: Multi-scale reduces MSCE in low-density regions while maintaining high-density performance.

title: Ablation: Cold-Start Strategy
description: Test contribution of hierarchical warm-up protocol.

steps.step1: Implement SMAK-S-NoWarmup: Disable warmup. Enable adaptation immediately from t=1.
steps.step2: Run on 2 datasets: synthetic_hetero, synthetic_density. Focus on first 200 steps.
steps.step3: Use seed 0 only.
steps.step4: Track: coverage violations (|coverage - 0.9| > 0.05) in first 200 steps.
steps.step6: Test: Warmup reduces early coverage violations by >50%.

title: Ablation: Number of Scales K
description: Analyze sensitivity to number of scales in the hierarchy.

steps.step1: Run SMAK-S with K ∈ {2, 3, 4, 5} scales. Adjust rho to maintain similar bandwidth range.
steps.step2: Test on synthetic_density dataset only (single dataset for sensitivity).
steps.step3: Use seed 0 only.
steps.step4: Measure: MSCE, avg set width, runtime per step.
steps.step6: Identify tradeoff between coverage quality and computational cost.

title: Compute Statistical Significance Tests
description: Perform paired statistical tests to verify significant improvements over baselines.

steps.step1: Aggregate results across seeds for each method-dataset pair. Compute mean and std for: marginal_coverage, MSCE, avg_width, WSC.
steps.step2: Perform paired t-tests: SMAK-S vs each baseline on each dataset for MSCE and avg_width. Use scipy.stats.ttest_rel.
steps.step3: Report: t-statistic, p-value. Flag significant at p < 0.05.
steps.step4: Save to results/statistical_tests.json.
steps.step5: Verify: MSCE significantly lower than fixed-bandwidth methods (p < 0.05).

title: Analyze Coverage Convergence and Stability
description: Detailed analysis of coverage behavior over time to validate theoretical convergence claims.

steps.step1: For each method, compute rolling coverage in 100-step windows.
steps.step2: Compute coverage deviation: |rolling_coverage - 0.9|.
steps.step3: Compute: mean deviation, max deviation, convergence time (first window where deviation < 0.03 and stays < 0.05 for 5 windows).
steps.step4: For synthetic_drift, compute coverage per segment. Measure recovery time after each drift point.

title: Local Coverage Analysis by Data Density
description: Analyze conditional coverage performance in different data density regions.

steps.step1: For each dataset, compute local data density using k-NN with k=50.
steps.step2: Bin points into 3 density regions: low (bottom 25%), medium (25-75%), high (top 25%).
steps.step3: Compute coverage and average set width for each region separately for each method.
steps.step4: Create comparison table: method × density_region with coverage and width metrics.
steps.step5: Test: SMAK-CP maintains more consistent coverage across density regions vs fixed-bandwidth.

title: Runtime and Computational Efficiency Analysis
description: Measure and compare computational cost of all methods.

steps.step1: Extract runtime per prediction for each method from saved results.
steps.step2: Compute: mean runtime per step (ms), relative slowdown vs Split CP.
steps.step3: Profile SMAK-CP components: kernel computation, quantile estimation, bandwidth adaptation, scale selection.
steps.step4: Test: SMAK-CP runtime within 2× of single-scale methods.

title: Generate Coverage and Width Trajectory Plots
description: Create time-series plots showing coverage and set width evolution for paper figures.

steps.step1: Load results for: Split CP, ACI, Fixed-Kernel, SMAK-S on synthetic_drift dataset.
steps.step2: Figure 1: Coverage trajectory. X-axis: time. Y-axis: rolling coverage (100-step window). Line at 0.9. Vertical lines at drift points. 4 methods with colors. Shaded 95% CI across seeds.
steps.step3: Figure 2: Set width trajectory. Same format, Y-axis is average set width.
steps.step4: Figure 3: Bandwidth adaptation for SMAK-S. X-axis: time. Y-axis: base bandwidth h_t^(1).

title: Generate Comparative Performance Bar Charts
description: Create bar charts comparing methods across datasets and metrics.

steps.step1: Figure 4: MSCE comparison. X-axis: datasets (6). Y-axis: MSCE. Grouped bars for 5 methods. Error bars show std across seeds. Significance indicators (* for p<0.05).
steps.step2: Figure 5: Average set width comparison. Same format. Lower is better.
steps.step3: Figure 6: Runtime comparison. Y-axis: relative runtime (Split CP = 1). Log scale.
steps.step4: Save to figures/fig4_msce_comparison.pdf, figures/fig5_width_comparison.pdf, figures/fig6_runtime_comparison.pdf.

title: Generate Ablation Study Visualizations
description: Create figures showing contribution of each component.

steps.step1: Figure 7: Cold-start comparison. X-axis: first 200 steps. Y-axis: |coverage - 0.9|. Two lines: with/without warmup.
steps.step2: Figure 8: Multi-scale benefit by density. X-axis: density region (low, medium, high). Y-axis: MSCE. Compare SMAK-S vs Single-Scale.
steps.step3: Figure 9: Number of scales sensitivity. X-axis: K (2,3,4,5). Y-axis: MSCE (left) and runtime (right).
steps.step4: Save to figures/fig7_warmup_ablation.pdf, figures/fig8_multiscale_ablation.pdf, figures/fig9_kscales_ablation.pdf.

title: Generate Summary Tables for Paper
description: Create formatted tables with all key results for inclusion in paper.

steps.step1: Table 1: Main results. Rows: methods (5). Columns: Marginal Coverage, MSCE, WSC, Avg Width, Runtime. Report mean ± std across datasets and seeds. Best in bold.
steps.step2: Table 2: Dataset-specific results for SMAK-S. Rows: datasets (6). Columns: Coverage, MSCE, Width.
steps.step3: Table 3: Ablation summary. Rows: ablation conditions. Columns: relevant metrics.
steps.step4: Save as CSV: figures/table1_main.csv, figures/table2_datasets.csv, figures/table3_ablation.csv.
steps.step5: Also save LaTeX versions: figures/table1_main.tex, etc.

title: Final Summary and Success Criteria Verification
description: Summarize all results and verify success criteria from proposal.

steps.step1: Verify Criterion 1: Target marginal coverage (0.9 ± 0.02). Check SMAK-S coverage across all datasets. Report pass/fail.
steps.step2: Verify Criterion 2: MSCE significantly lower than fixed-bandwidth. Report p-value. Pass if p < 0.05.
steps.step3: Verify Criterion 3: 10-30% tighter prediction sets than Fixed-Kernel. Compute width reduction. Pass if within range.
steps.step4: Verify Criterion 4: Runtime within 2× of single-scale. Report relative runtime. Pass if < 2.0.
steps.step5: Verify Criterion 5: Cold-start reduces violations by >50%. Compare warmup vs no-warmup in first 200 steps. Pass if reduction > 50%.
steps.step7: Document any failures honestly with analysis.
